name: El Salvador

colors.symbolism: the two oceans bordering Central America and the country's commitment to truth and justice
the territory between the two oceans, peace, unity and prosperity
the radiating sun within the coat of arms
the laurel wreath within the coat of arms
the Phrygian cap within the coat of arms

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — cobalt blue, white, cobalt blue — with the national coat of arms centred on the white band: a triangle enclosing five volcanoes between two seas, a Phrygian cap radiating golden rays, and a rainbow, framed by a laurel wreath of fourteen branches and the country's name and motto.
history: The blue-white-blue scheme derives from the flag of the Federal Republic of Central America (1823–1839) and was re-adopted in El Salvador on 17 May 1912 by legislative decree, replacing several earlier designs in use during the late 19th century. The flag is celebrated nationally on 'Día de la Bandera Salvadoreña' on 17 May; it is closely related in design to the flags of Nicaragua, Honduras and Argentina.

features.symbolism: the two blue bands represent the Pacific Ocean and the Caribbean Sea, while the white centre band stands for the land between them and the peace of the nation; the design is shared with the historic flag of the Federal Republic of Central America

an equilateral triangle (equality, justice) containing five volcanoes (the five member-states of the Federal Republic of Central America) rising from a sea, a Phrygian cap of liberty radiating golden rays above, and a rainbow; surrounded by a laurel wreath of fourteen branches (the country's fourteen departments) and the inscription 'REPUBLICA DE EL SALVADOR EN LA AMERICA CENTRAL', with the motto 'DIOS UNION LIBERTAD' below
